headline: Homemaker, Silver Spring MD
IT Director, Pinnacle Federal Solutions
Family member, Bethesda MD
Pediatrician, Chesapeake Children's Medical Center
Son-in-law, Baltimore MD
Student, Silverbrook Academy; USCF 2050 chess player
Student, Bethesda MD
Student, Baltimore MD
Student, Baltimore MD
Retired Political Science Professor, Chesapeake State University
Vice President, MACCF; Mathematics Professor, Chesapeake State University
Senior Pastor, Grace Community Church
President, Silver Spring Community Association
Primary Care Physician, Silver Spring Family Medicine
Endocrinologist, Capital Endocrine Associates
Chess Federation Director, Richmond VA
Cardiologist, Capital Cardiology Associates
Orthopedic Surgeon, Silver Spring Orthopedics
Podiatrist, Silver Spring MD
Family member, Richmond VA
Deacon, Grace Community Church
Opening study from 7 to 8:30 — Sicilian Defense lines again.
MACCF board meeting second Saturday; the youth program at six schools.
Saturday chess and lemonade with Bill in Takoma Park, decades on.
Annual Open at the Silver Spring Civic Center in June, 80 to 120 players.
Sunday lunch with Eric and Diane, the family filling the house.
Played Tony Jr. — wanting to win and to lose to a boy who has surpassed me.
Sligo Creek walk, a mile and a half each way, thinking time.
The memoir at sixty pages, drafted slowly; published or simply written, unclear.
Chili for the church potluck — legendary, if I may say so.
Coordinated the exchange program with the Virginia federation.
Metformin with dinner, fasting glucose logged on the Accu-Chek.
Beethoven this evening; which composer is a fair read of the weather.
Federation correspondence with Dr. Crawford in Richmond.
Teaching the twins the basics — knight before bishop.
Foreign Affairs quarterly arrived; the McCullough essays a third time.
Board spreadsheet updated for the twelve clubs across the region.
Garden tomatoes and peppers coming in, watched like negotiation drafts.
Tea with milk and sugar, three cups, never coffee.
Arsenal on the weekend, a habit from the London posting.
Civic-event coordination with Nancy at the community association.
Cataract follow-up done; I prefer large text and good lighting now.
Tony Jr. at 2050 USCF, headed to college in the fall of 2027.
Pastor Olson's counsel after service — belief as obligation.
Withings BP logged, Oura readiness modest, the morning quiet.
